channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Mourinho in final negotiations to become Real manager
item.description: Jose Mourinho is in final negotiations to become Real Madrid's next head coach, 13 years after his first spell at the Bernabeu.

item.title: White out for season and set to miss World Cup
item.description: Arsenal and England defender Ben White is set to miss the rest of the season with a knee injury.

item.title: It's West Ham or Spurs - how relegation fight is shaping up
item.description: With two games remaining of the Premier League season, Tottenham and West Ham are the two horses left in the race to avoid relegation.

item.title: Tuchel submits 55-man provisional World Cup squad
item.description: Thomas Tuchel submits his provisional England squad for the World Cup, with Luke Shaw, Danny Welbeck and Alex Scott believed to be among the players named.

item.title: Advocaat, 78, set for World Cup record after Curacao return
item.description: Dick Advocaat makes a surprise return as Curacao's head coach after his successor Fred Rutten resigned just a month out from their World Cup debut.

item.title: O'Neill turns down Blackburn job to remain with NI
item.description: Michael O'Neill will not be taking the Blackburn Rovers job on a permanent basis and will remain in his role as Northern Ireland manager.

item.title: How do Chelsea rebuild for next season's WSL?
item.description: Chelsea are in an unusual position with one game to go in the Women's Super League and no silverware on the line.

item.title: Isak and Gyokeres named in Sweden World Cup squad
item.description: Arsenal striker Viktor Gyokeres and Liverpool forward Alexander Isak are named in Sweden's World Cup squad.

item.title: McInnes and O'Neill keeping cool as Premiership title race catches fire
item.description: It seems as though everyone is hyping up the conclusion to the Scottish Premiership title race. Everyone except those involved, that is.

item.title: Southampton ask for more time over spying claims
item.description: Southampton request more time to conduct an internal review after being charged with spying on Championship play-off rivals Middlesbrough.

item.title: Scotland to play Israel behind closed doors in Hungary
item.description: Scotland's Women's World Cup qualifying double-header against Israel next month will be played behind closed doors at a neutral stadium in Hungary.

item.title: Barnsley reappoint Stendel as head coach
item.description: Barnsley reappoint Daniel Stendel, who led the club to promotion in 2019, as head coach following Conor Hourihane's departure.

item.title: Slavia Prague punished for pitch invasion
item.description: Slavia Prague are fined after a mass pitch invasion sees their match against rivals Sparta Prague abandoned

item.title: Calvert-Lewin penalty earns Leeds draw at Tottenham
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur squander the opportunity to take a significant step towards Premier League safety as they are held to a 1-1 draw at home by Leeds United.

item.title: Watch: Mead marks 50th Arsenal goal with stunning double
item.description: Watch Beth Mead score her 50th and 51st WSL goals for Arsenal with the England forward set to depart the club at the end of the season.

item.title: Tottenham players good enough to stay up - De Zerbi
item.description: Tottenham boss Roberto De Zerbi says his players are good enough to stay up after their draw with Leeds United leaves them two points clear above the relegation zone with two games to play.

item.title: Farke proud of 'resilient' Leeds after Spurs draw
item.description: Leeds boss Daniel Farke is pleased with the "mentality" and "resilience" shown by his side as they earned a 1-1 draw away at Tottenham thanks to Dominic Calvert-Lewin's 74th-minute penalty.

item.title: 'Gutted but proud' - Millwall's season of progress
item.description: Millwall's Championship season ends in play-off semi-final defeat against Hull City but the Lions have made progress, former striker Steve Morison says.

item.title: Wales confident Bellamy will stay amid Burnley links
item.description: Football Association of Wales bosses are "very confident" Craig Bellamy will remain head coach of the national team despite interest from Burnley.

item.title: Slavia Prague fined £357,000 after derby abandoned
item.description: Slavia Prague have been fined 10m Czech crowns (£357,000), ordered to play four games behind closed doors and had their match against Sparta Prague forfeited after the derby had to be abandoned.

item.title: Barcelona manager Flick agrees new contract
item.description: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick agrees a contract extension after leading the Spanish giants to a 29th La Liga title.

item.title: Bale more 'complete' than Ronaldo - Casemiro
item.description: Manchester United's former Real Madrid midfielder Casemiro says Cristiano Ronaldo is the greatest footballer he has played with - but that Gareth Bale is the "most complete".

item.title: 'No question mark' over Rohl's Rangers future after board talks
item.description: Danny Rohl says he has been assured of his position as Rangers head coach by the Ibrox board.

item.title: Stormzy's film company to co-produce Wright biopic
item.description: The film will explore the early life of the ex-footballer and former Match of The Day pundit.

item.title: Forty years of the EFL play-offs - choose your favourite memories
item.description: BBC Sport looks back at 15 memorable moments from 40 years of the play-offs in the English Football League.

item.title: Three clubs eyeing Bowen - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: Multiple clubs interested in West Ham captain Jarrod Bowen, Liverpool eye Monaco duo Maghnes Akliouche and Lamine Camara, and Juventus considering a loan move for Nicolas Jackson.

item.title: 'Superhuman' managers shouldn't have to ask for help - Beard's family on loss
item.description: The family of former WSL manager Matt Beard tell BBC Sport about the emotional burden of football management, and the changes they want to see.

item.title: What is Scotland's most iconic goal? This time - Griffiths v Gemmill
item.description: Vote to find out what goal will be crowned Scotland's greatest.

item.title: 'One of saves of the season' - Kinsky's redemption moment at Tottenham?
item.description: Two months after many thought Antonin Kinsky would never play for Tottenham again the goalkeeper makes one of the saves of the season to earn Spurs a point in their fight for Premier League survival.

item.title: Five 'fouls' in one move - breaking down Premier League corner chaos
item.description: The Premier League season has been defined by set-piece drama. It came to a head at West Ham on Sunday, when the VAR could have penalised any one of a handful of potential fouls.

item.title: 'Brilliant' Belloumi turns Jakirovic's displeasure into delight
item.description: Hull City winger Mohamed Belloumi made up for his lack of preparedness to win the Championship play-off semi-final against Millwall for his side.

item.title: What does future hold for Barcelona forward Rashford?
item.description: Marcus Rashford has an uncertain future despite the on-loan Manchester United forward scoring a brilliant free-kick for Barcelona in El Clasico.

item.title: How to follow the Premier League on the BBC this weekend
item.description: BBC Sport Ask Me Anything explains how to follow the Premier League on the BBC this weekend.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: Rashford goal helps Barca beat Real Madrid to lift title
item.description: Watch highlights as Barcelona beat Real Madrid in El Clasico to secure back-to-back La Liga titles.

item.title: Arsenal edge closer to title after late West Ham drama
item.description: Leandro Trossard's 83rd-minute goal is enough to give Premier League leaders Arsenal a dramatic 1-0 win, as West Ham United are denied a stoppage-time equaliser by VAR at the London Stadium.

item.title: Watch: 'Genius' overhead kick from Celtic's Maeda
item.description: Daizen Maeda conjures up a moment of magic to seal Celtic's 3-1 win over Rangers.

item.title: Anderson scores late equaliser as Forest move closer to safety
item.description: Elliot Anderson scores against his former club to earn Nottingham Forest a 1-1 draw against Newcastle United and a seven-point cushion from the Premier League relegation zone.

item.title: VAR's biggest ever decision? - analysis
item.description: MOTD pundits Shay Given and Danny Murphy, and former Premier League assistant referee Darren Cann, analyse the controversial decision to rule out West Ham's late equaliser against Arsenal.

item.title: Burnley end losing streak with draw against Villa
item.description: Burnley end a run of five-straight Premier League defeats as they draw 2-2 with Aston Villa at Turf Moor.

item.title: Everton's European push dented with draw at Palace
item.description: Everton's push to finish in a European spot suffer a setback as Crystal Palace twice come from behind to pick up a 2-2 draw at Selhurst Park.

item.title: How Flick's reshaped Barca dominated La Liga again
item.description: Hansi Flick has guided a new style of Barcelona to back-to-back La Liga titles - this is how the German coach did it.

item.title: 'It breaks my heart' - legend Mead to leave Arsenal
item.description: Beth Mead says it "breaks her heart" to leave Arsenal after it is announced the forward will depart the club at the end of the season.

item.title: Chelsea start contacting prospective head coaches
item.description: Chelsea are starting to make contact with prospective managerial candidates as they move towards appointing the club's next permanent head coach, including Andoni Iraola, Xabi Alonso, Marco Silva, and Oliver Glasner.

item.title: Liverpool release images of planned Jota memorial
item.description: Liverpool release images of a planned memorial to late forward Diogo Jota and his brother Andre Silva, following their deaths last summer.

item.title: Biggest VAR call ever? Four chaotic minutes that may define the season
item.description: The most consequential decision in the history of VAR in the Premier League is played out over four minutes 11 seconds of drama at London Stadium.

item.title: Bosnia's Dzeko to join list of World Cup 40-year-olds
item.description: Former Manchester City striker Edin Dzeko is set to join exclusive list of players aged over 40 to play in a World Cup.

item.title: Milne alleged racist abuse towards Bokila 'not proven', says SFA
item.description: An allegation of racist abuse by Aberdeen defender Jack Milne towards Livingston striker Jeremy Bokila is found "not proven" following a Scottish FA inquiry.

item.title: 'Only his last changes worked' - how Arteta's gambles finally paid off
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Danny Murphy explains why Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta deserves credit after his side beat West Ham to take a huge step towards the Premier League title.

item.title: The reasons West Ham face relegation - and why they are unlucky
item.description: When the dust settles on the West Ham goal that was ruled out against Arsenal, the cold reality is they are now probably facing relegation.

item.title: Lacking depth, little threat - Man Utd will not find replacing Casemiro easy
item.description: Manchester United have failed to win any of the four Premier League games Casemiro has failed to start this season, leaving Michael Carrick to ponder how to replace him when he leaves this summer.

item.title: Bonuses, players on the beach & the phoney war - Pulis' end-of-season insights
item.description: Tony Pulis on why motivating players who have nothing to play for is just one of the challenges facing a manager at the end of the season.

item.title: How Le Mans rose from sixth tier to cusp of top-flight return
item.description: Le Mans is most commonly known for its 24-hour race, but the city's football club is backed by big-name sportsmen and on a rapid rise.

item.title: Ups, downs and the race for Europe
item.description: All you need to know about promotion, relegation and qualification for Europe in England and Scotland's top men's football leagues, and the Women's Super League.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What are Fifa's rules on switching nationalities?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the rules on players switching nations in international football.

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Which Premier League players are out of contract this summer?
item.description: With a World Cup summer fast approaching, BBC Sport looks at which Premier League stars may be looking for new clubs.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Wilson plots way past old boss Lennon - watch Dunfermline v Partick Thistle on BBC
item.description: Partick Thistle manager Mark Wilson says there'll be "no nostalgia" when he faces his old captain and manager, Neil Lennon, in this week's Premiership play-off semi-final with Dunfermline Athletic.

item.title: Rangers ready to reignite Penrice interest - gossip
item.description: Rangers target a summer move for AEK Athens left-back James Penrice, while Martin Boyle's former teammate would not be surprised to see him join Aberdeen.

item.title: Forest Women part company with boss Davies
item.description: Nottingham Forest Women part company with head coach Carly Davies after finishing seventh in WSL 2.

item.title: Why Brighton's first Women's FA Cup final felt like it was coming
item.description: Brighton reach their first Women's FA Cup final - and within one victory of a first piece of major silverware in the club's history.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
